CheckTitle: MySQL flexible server has geo-redundant backup enabled
Description: **Azure MySQL Flexible Server** is evaluated for **geo-redundant backup**. Geo-redundant backup stores copies in a paired Azure region, enabling recovery from a full regional outage.
Risk: Without geo-redundant backup, a regional disaster causes **permanent data loss**. Locally redundant backups only protect against storage hardware failures within the same region.

Code.Other:
1. Sign in to Azure portal
2. Go to Azure Database for MySQL flexible servers
3. Select the server
4. Go to Settings > Backup and restore
5. Enable Geo-redundant backup
6. Click Save
Note: Geo-redundant backup can only be set during server creation for some tiers.
Code.Terraform:
```hcl
resource "azurerm_mysql_flexible_server" "<example_resource_name>" {
  name                         = "<example_resource_name>"
  location                     = "<example_resource_id>"
  resource_group_name          = "<example_resource_id>"
  geo_redundant_backup_enabled = true  # Critical: enables geo-redundant backup
  administrator_login          = "adminuser"
  administrator_password       = "<admin_password>"
  sku_name                     = "GP_Standard_D2ds_v4"
}
```

Recommendation.Text: Enable **geo-redundant backup** on MySQL Flexible Server to store backup copies in a paired Azure region. This provides cross-region disaster recovery capability. Note: geo-redundant backup can only be configured during server **creation** for some SKU tiers. Plan server provisioning accordingly.
